description: In 2014, Pavel Durov, the founder of VKontakte (VK), was ousted following pressure from the Russian government to provide data on activists. Users debated whether to remain on the platform or migrate to Telegram, which Durov launched as a privacy-focused alternative.

key_events.description: Pavel Durov is forced to resign as VK CEO.
Telegram gains massive adoption among Russian youth and political activists.
VK is integrated into the Mail.ru group, tightening state control.
Network effect keeps the majority of general users on VK despite privacy concerns.

calibration_notes: Highlights the tension between political ideology and the convenience of network effects.
